security_analysis.explanation: The website uses HTTP instead of HTTPS — meaning the connection is NOT encrypted.
security_analysis.risk: Everything you type on this site (passwords, card numbers) is sent in plain text. Anyone on the same network can intercept it.

security_analysis.explanation: A large portion of the URL consists of numbers.
security_analysis.risk: Randomly generated or machine-created phishing URLs tend to have lots of numbers to avoid pattern-based detection systems.

security_analysis.explanation: The URL contains repeated character sequences (e.g. "aaaa", "xxxxx").
security_analysis.risk: Attackers use repeated characters to pad URLs, confuse automated scanners, or fill space in obfuscated fake addresses.

label: legitimate
reasons: Unusually high number of input fields (23)

security_analysis.explanation: The URL contains phishing keywords like "login", "verify", "secure", "admin", "account", "update".
security_analysis.risk: Attackers deliberately use these words to make fake pages look like official login or account management pages of real brands.

security_analysis.explanation: The URL is unusually long compared to normal websites.
security_analysis.risk: Attackers make URLs very long to hide the real destination, overflow browser displays, and confuse security scanners.

security_analysis.explanation: The URL contains many hyphens (-).
security_analysis.risk: Multiple hyphens are used as visual tricks to make fake domains look like real brand names at a quick glance.

security_analysis.explanation: The URL uses a raw IP address (like 192.168.1.1) instead of a domain name.

security_analysis.explanation: The website uses HTTP instead of HTTPS — meaning the connection is NOT encrypted.
security_analysis.risk: Everything you type on this site (passwords, card numbers) is sent in plain text. Anyone on the same network can intercept it.

security_analysis.explanation: The URL contains phishing keywords like "login", "verify", "secure", "admin", "account", "update".
security_analysis.risk: Attackers deliberately use these words to make fake pages look like official login or account management pages of real brands.

security_analysis.explanation: The domain name contains a hyphen (e.g. paypal-login.com, secure-bank.net).
security_analysis.risk: Attackers add hyphens to make fake domains look similar to real brands and trick users into trusting them.

security_analysis.explanation: The URL contains repeated character sequences (e.g. "aaaa", "xxxxx").
security_analysis.risk: Attackers use repeated characters to pad URLs, confuse automated scanners, or fill space in obfuscated fake addresses.

security_analysis.explanation: The average length of words in the full URL is unusually short.
security_analysis.risk: Machine-generated phishing domains often use very short or meaningless word fragments that are hard to read or remember.

label: suspicious
reasons: Suspicious keywords in page title: "Sign in – Google accounts"

security_analysis.explanation: The URL uses a raw IP address (like 192.168.1.1) instead of a domain name.
security_analysis.risk: Real websites almost never use raw IP addresses. Attackers use IPs to avoid domain blacklists and hide their identity.

security_analysis.explanation: The URL uses a link shortener like bit.ly, tinyurl, or similar services.
security_analysis.risk: Shortened links completely hide the real destination. You cannot know where you are being sent before clicking.

security_analysis.explanation: The URL contains phishing keywords like "login", "verify", "secure", "admin", "account", "update".
security_analysis.risk: Attackers deliberately use these words to make fake pages look like official login or account management pages of real brands.

security_analysis.explanation: The URL is unusually long compared to normal websites.
security_analysis.risk: Attackers make URLs very long to hide the real destination, overflow browser displays, and confuse security scanners.

security_analysis.explanation: The URL contains an unusually large number of dots (.).
security_analysis.risk: More dots mean more subdomains. Attackers stack subdomains like "apple.paypal.secure.login.evil.com" to make fake sites look real.

security_analysis.explanation: The URL contains many hyphens (-).
security_analysis.risk: Multiple hyphens are used as visual tricks to make fake domains look like real brand names at a quick glance.

security_analysis.explanation: A large portion of the URL consists of numbers.
security_analysis.risk: Randomly generated or machine-created phishing URLs tend to have lots of numbers to avoid pattern-based detection systems.

security_analysis.explanation: The URL contains repeated character sequences (e.g. "aaaa", "xxxxx").
security_analysis.risk: Attackers use repeated characters to pad URLs, confuse automated scanners, or fill space in obfuscated fake addresses.

security_analysis.risk: Attackers make URLs very long to hide the real destination, overflow browser displays, and confuse security scanners.

security_analysis.explanation: The URL contains an unusually large number of dots (.).
security_analysis.risk: More dots mean more subdomains. Attackers stack subdomains like "apple.paypal.secure.login.evil.com" to make fake sites look real.

security_analysis.explanation: The URL contains many hyphens (-).
security_analysis.risk: Multiple hyphens are used as visual tricks to make fake domains look like real brand names at a quick glance.

security_analysis.explanation: The URL contains percent-encoded characters (%) used to hide its true content.
security_analysis.risk: Percent encoding can disguise malicious characters, bypass URL filters, or hide the real destination of a redirect.

security_analysis.explanation: A large portion of the URL consists of numbers.
security_analysis.risk: Randomly generated or machine-created phishing URLs tend to have lots of numbers to avoid pattern-based detection systems.

security_analysis.explanation: The URL contains repeated character sequences (e.g. "aaaa", "xxxxx").
security_analysis.risk: Attackers use repeated characters to pad URLs, confuse automated scanners, or fill space in obfuscated fake addresses.

security_analysis.explanation: The website uses HTTP instead of HTTPS — meaning the connection is NOT encrypted.
security_analysis.risk: Everything you type on this site (passwords, card numbers) is sent in plain text. Anyone on the same network can intercept it.
